islands.name: Phosphor Reef
islands.description: 1970s wireframe seas — glowing green markets. Navigate inflation spikes while your FUEL gauge tracks cashflow.

learning_objectives: Understand how credit scores are built and why they matter
Learn the basics of investing: risk, return, and diversification
Read simple market indicators like price trends and volume
Distinguish between responsible borrowing and debt traps
provenance.originality_notes: Theme: cyberpunk-inspired signal-tower city (not a trading floor or bank). Verb: decode market signals via NPC intel missions and build a mock portfolio (not day-trading simulation). Structure: investigation-style quests where players gather clues about credit and investing before making decisions (not competitive trading). Combines detective-style NPC quests with simplified portfolio-building puzzles.

areas.name: Signal Plaza
areas.description: The city center. Giant screens flash market tickers and credit tips. A street mentor hangs out by the fountain.

areas.name: Credit Lane
areas.description: A narrow street lined with lending offices and score-tracking kiosks. Learn what makes a score rise or fall.

areas.name: Investor Tower
areas.description: A glass skyscraper where analysts study trends. The observation deck shows the whole market at a glance.

items.name: Market Pass
items.description: Grants access to Investor Tower. Earned by proving you understand credit basics.

items.name: Credit Report
items.description: Your personal credit snapshot. Shows what actions raise or lower your score.

items.name: Signal City Badge
items.description: Proof you can read market signals and manage credit responsibly.

quests.title: Credit 101
quests.description: Meet Mentor Max to learn what credit is, then visit Credit Cleo to see how scores work and earn your Market Pass.
quests.hint: Start at Signal Plaza, then head to Credit Lane.

quests.title: First Portfolio
quests.description: Use your Market Pass to enter Investor Tower. Meet Analyst Ari and Broker Blake to build a practice portfolio.
quests.hint: You need the Market Pass from Credit Lane before entering Investor Tower.

nodes.speaker: Mentor Max
nodes.text: Welcome to Signal City, where every blinking light tells a story about money. Credit and investing can seem complicated, but I'll break it down. Ready?

nodes.speaker: Mentor Max
nodes.text: Credit is borrowed money you promise to pay back. Your credit score tracks how reliably you keep that promise. Head to Credit Lane and talk to Cleo — she knows scores inside and out.

nodes.speaker: Mentor Max
nodes.text: Take your time. The signals will still be here when you're ready.

nodes.speaker: Credit Cleo
nodes.text: Your credit score goes UP when you pay bills on time and keep balances low. It goes DOWN when you miss payments or borrow too much. Simple as that!

choices.text: Can I see my credit report?

nodes.speaker: Credit Cleo
nodes.text: Here you go! Keep an eye on it. Good habits now mean better options later — lower interest rates, easier approvals. Try the Signal Scanner minigame to test your knowledge!

nodes.speaker: Credit Cleo
nodes.text: Scores range from 300 to 850. Above 700 is considered good. The higher your score, the more trustworthy lenders think you are.

nodes.speaker: Analyst Ari
nodes.text: Welcome to Investor Tower! Investing means putting money into something now, hoping it grows over time. The key is diversification — don't put all your eggs in one basket.

nodes.speaker: Analyst Ari
nodes.text: Spread your investments across different types — some safer, some riskier. That way, if one drops, the others can balance it out. Talk to Broker Blake to try building a practice portfolio!

nodes.speaker: Analyst Ari
nodes.text: Every investment carries risk — you could lose money. But historically, diversified investments tend to grow over long periods. The trick is patience and balance.

nodes.speaker: Broker Blake
nodes.text: Ready to build your first portfolio? I've got three mock asset types: steady bonds, growth stocks, and balanced funds. How you mix them is up to you!

nodes.speaker: Broker Blake
nodes.text: Great attitude! Remember: bonds are steady but grow slowly, stocks can grow fast but swing up and down, and balanced funds sit in the middle. Pick a mix that feels right for you.

nodes.speaker: Broker Blake
nodes.text: Bonds are the safest, but they grow the slowest. If you're young, you have time to handle some risk — that's when growth stocks make sense. Think long-term!

minigames.name: Signal Scanner
minigames.description: Read credit and market signals on screen and answer quick-fire questions about what each signal means.

minigames.name: Don't Break the Basket
minigames.description: Spread your coins across assets, then weather a live market year. Concentrate and a crash stings; diversify and you ride it out.
